reforms.description: The Federal Government adopted a programme to encourage the establishment of new firms by young entrepreneurs, providing personal loans without the usual security requirements from banks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging new firm establishment supports innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been adopted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: While it supports startups, it is not a major structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: Federal Government adopted programme to encourage setting up of new firms by young entrepreneurs, effective 14th February; under it a contribution of up to DM 100000 to be given towards starting capital in the form of personal loans without security normally required by banks.

reforms.description: The Programme to Strengthen Demand and Improve Growth was introduced, amounting to DM 16 billion, aimed at stimulating economic activity and supporting public investment. Additionally, the Second Revision of the Energy Programme introduced a retroactive tax credit and grant programme for homeowners under the Housing Modernisation Act, amounting to DM 4.35 billion over five years. An amendment to the Investment Allowance Act of 1975 was also introduced to provide a 7.5 percent allowance for investment in combined heat and power plants, industrial waste recovery, and district heating systems.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to stimulate economic activity and support investments, which can enhance innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programmes have been adopted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scale and impact of these programmes represent a significant reform in economic policy.
reforms.source_quote: The main features being: a retroactive tax credit and grant programme for home owners under the Housing Modernisation Act. The programme started in July 1978 and amounts to DM 4.35 billion over five years.
